summary: Set 022 Session 3 (final session): documentation + cross-consumer verification. Three docs updated to encode the state-first lifecycle protocol Sessions 1 (ai_router 0.2.3) and 2 (extension v0.13.12) shipped. (1) docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md: Step 1 gained 'State first, work second (Set 022)' with the canonical three-line invariant and both Full-tier CLI and Lightweight-tier hand-write pseudo-code; Step 8 gained 'Symmetric close protocol (Set 022)' with the non-final-vs-final field-change table. (2) docs/session-state-schema.md: added a top-level 'State invariant (Set 022 — canonical)' section, added completedSessions[] to the required-fields JSON example, promoted completedSessions[] from 'optional but planned' to 'always written (Full) / always maintained (Lightweight)' with explicit writer responsibilities, updated parser cheat-sheet to drop the retired currentSession-1 reader fallback and add the events-ledger fallback step, added an in-flight predicate computation block, updated the Full-tier mid-set worked example to carry completedSessions[1], and made the array required on both tiers in the migration section. (3) ai_router/docs/close-out.md: added Section 0 'Session-boundary writes (start and close)' with field-by-field tables for both writers, idempotency notes, and tier-symmetry callouts; extended Section 5 drift-case-1 to mention completedSessions[] backfill on --repair --apply. Cross-consumer verification (read-only) walked all three consumer repos and confirmed no session set will break on next boundary write: 13 Full-tier sets across dabbler-platform (8) and dabbler-access-harvester (5) will heal cleanly via the events-ledger fallback; 58 Lightweight-tier sets continue under hand-maintenance with no warning fired in normal operation; 6 dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb sets are mixed compliance (4 numeric arrays + 2 string-array sets in terminal Lightweight state — surfaced as a non-blocking follow-up for the consumer repo). Cross-provider verification (api method): round 1 ISSUES_FOUND with 2 Major findings in the homehealthcare narrative ($0.096); round 2 ISSUES_FOUND with 1 Minor finding on Lightweight stderr-warning precision ($0.163); round 3 VERIFIED, issues: [] ($0.159). All findings were in the verification narrative, not in the doc edits themselves. Total Session 3 verification spend: $0.4175; cumulative Set 022 verification spend (all three sessions): ~$0.99. This is the final session — close_session will write change-log.md and flip status to 'complete' + lifecycleState to 'closed' (len(completedSessions) == totalSessions == 3 post-append).
